stories.title: Apple raises prices of MacBooks, iPads as memory costs skyrocket
stories.summary: Apple has increased prices across its MacBook and iPad lineups, citing soaring memory chip costs driven by AI infrastructure demand. The price hikes reflect the broader impact of the AI hardware boom on consumer electronics supply chains.

stories.title: Google integrates 'Computer Use' into Gemini 3.5 Flash
stories.summary: Google has integrated autonomous computer control directly into Gemini 3.5 Flash, enabling the model to operate browsers, desktops, and mobile devices. It scores 78.4 on the OSWorld benchmark, marking a major step toward truly agentic AI.

stories.title: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 achieves 20x faster programming for robodogs
stories.summary: Anthropic's latest Claude Opus 4.7 model demonstrated 20x faster programming performance for robotic dogs compared to the best human engineering team, showcasing the accelerating capability of AI in embodied robotics.

stories.title: The unbearable cheapness of open weight models
stories.summary: An analysis of how the rapid proliferation of open-weight AI models is driving down costs and reshaping the competitive landscape. The piece examines the economic implications for both open-source communities and proprietary model vendors.

stories.title: Show HN: OpenKnowledge – open source AI-first alternative to Obsidian/Notion
stories.summary: OpenKnowledge is a new open-source, AI-native knowledge management tool designed as an alternative to Obsidian and Notion. Built by Inkeep, it aims to integrate AI deeply into personal knowledge workflows.

stories.title: I built a GPU backend for Emacs
stories.summary: A developer detailed their journey building a GPU-accelerated rendering backend for the Emacs text editor, potentially enabling dramatically faster rendering and opening the door to richer visual interfaces in the classic editor.

stories.title: AI-designed universal vaccine passes first human trial
stories.summary: Researchers at the University of Cambridge announced that an AI-designed vaccine has successfully completed initial human trials — the first time a vaccine's key component was designed entirely by artificial intelligence.

stories.title: Political bias in AI: Where the AI models stand
stories.summary: A comprehensive analysis mapping the political leanings of major AI models, sparking heated discussion about neutrality, alignment, and the influence of training data on model outputs.

stories.title: Qualcomm in talks to acquire AI chip maker Tenstorrent for $8-10B
stories.summary: Qualcomm is in early acquisition talks with AI chip startup Tenstorrent, founded by Jim Keller, in a deal valued at $8-10 billion. The move would significantly bolster Qualcomm's position in the AI hardware race against Nvidia and AMD.

stories.title: OpenAI leans toward waiting until next year for IPO
stories.summary: OpenAI is reportedly considering delaying its highly anticipated IPO until next year, signaling the company wants more time to stabilize its corporate structure and demonstrate sustainable revenue growth.
